informal: If $x, y$ are complex, prove that $||x|-|y|| \leq |x-y|$.
reasoning: The informal statement asserts ||x|-|y|| ≤ |x-y| for complex x, y. The Lean signature takes x y : ℂ, uses ‖·‖ (the norm on ℂ, which coincides with the complex modulus |·|) for the moduli of x, y, and x-y, and uses |·| (real absolute value) for the outer absolute value of the real difference ‖x‖ - ‖y‖. This matches the informal claim exactly in quantifier structure, types, and conclusion.
